pattern: Because every parent equals the minimum of its children, the overall minimum is the root value, so the answer is just the smallest value strictly larger than the root anywhere in the tree.
hints: The smallest value in this special tree is always the root, so you are really hunting for the next value up from it.
Scan all node values and keep the smallest one that is strictly greater than the root's value.
Traverse the whole tree tracking a candidate; if no value ever exceeds the root, report that none exists.

edge_inputs.description: second smallest sits on the deeper branch
all values identical, no second minimum
second smallest appears in two different places
only one child subtree contains a larger value
second smallest is far from the smallest value
near-maximum values where the gap is one

stress.description: recursively grow a valid special tree where each parent is the min of its children
